rag_rules: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Delete invalid guarantees instead of adding architecture when references are unresolved
Align helper declaration types with expression types (bool for comparisons, int for integer operations, real for real operations)
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool in const and eq declarations
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
generic_rules: Trace the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper declaration on the same line for type errors
Use only visible identifiers in the current scope to avoid reference errors
Align assumed types with actual types in helper declarations
Delete invalid guarantees instead of adding architecture when references are unresolved
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
Place AGREE contracts inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool in const and eq declarations
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers, data types, or property references absent from the analyzed model and its dependencies
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports should be placed in valid component type scopes
Component type annexes are the normal location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
AADL package references require matching `with` clauses
Generated AGREE code should not introduce package-qualified identifiers, data types, or property references absent from the analyzed model and its configured dependencies
